body.title: N+1 PROCEDURAL scans: 30s TTL shared cache across push sources
body.result: Added get_cached_procedural(cortex) + invalidate_procedural_cache() at module level in push_sources.py. All 4 callers (HabitCandidateSource, ResourceMonitorSource, SelfObservationSource, CuriositySource) now share one PROCEDURAL scan per 30s instead of 4+ independent scans per minute. Cache uses time.monotonic() TTL, refreshes automatically on expiry, and can be force-invalidated on writes.
body.description: HabitCandidateSource, ResourceMonitorSource, SelfObservationSource, and CuriositySource all independently call cortex.get_by_type(PROCEDURAL) every 60-600s — 4+ full table scans per minute with no shared cache. At 10k+ memories this creates lock contention. Fix: add a module-level _procedural_cache dict in push_sources.py with TTL=30s, fetched once per loop cycle and shared by all sources. Invalidate on write. Performance finding from deep-audit Panels 1+6.
